rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al VS. CONTINENTAL CASUALTY COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Motion To Compel Deposition Of Pltf Joel Shamitoff
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 18, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT R.M.S. ASSOCIATES INC. D/B/A ROGER STONE INSURANCE, JOHN LOGUE Joinder In Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Joel Shamitoff. Defendant's motion to compel deposition of plaintiff Joel Shamitoff is granted. Plaintiff must present himself for deposition on or before September 15, 2011. Defendant's request for sanctions in the amount of $3000 is granted. Plaintiff must remit this amount to defendant's counsel by September 15, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al VS. CONTINENTAL CASUALTY COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatory Nos. 21 And 22 And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 18, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFF UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, JOEL SHAMITOFF, JOHN LUCERO Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatory Nos. 21 And 22 And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to interrogatories 21 and 22 is granted in part. Defendant is ordered to provide information of claims denied based on "Exclusion 1.d" for the years 2007-2009 and lawsuits stemming from a denial of a claim on "Exclusion 1.d" from 2005 to the present. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Compelling Pltf To Produce Documents

rulings.Case Title: IRA SERVICES INC. VS. AMERICAN ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses From Deft Zurich American Insurance Company To 1) First Amended Request For Production Of Documents And 2) First Set Of Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 18, 2011, line 2. PLAINTIFF IRA SERVICES INC. Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant Zurich American Insurance Company To 1) First Amended Request For Production Of Documents And 2) First Set Of Special Interrogatories. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to first amended request for production of documents and first set of special interrogatories is granted. The information sought by plaintiff relating to the loss reserves is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the issue of whether defendant conducted reasonable investigation, was following its own internal procedures for proper investigation and was acting in good or bad faith in denying plaintiff's claim. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ELSY M TADEO et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cal Western Reconveyance Corporation Motion For Joinder For Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 18, 2011, line 6. Defendant Cal Western Reconveyance Corporation Motion For Joinder For Demurrer. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 1, 2, and 7th causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. Collateral Estoppel precludes these claims and the related issues from being relitgated as they were adjudicated in the prior Unlawful Detainer action. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 3rd cause (Violation of CC §1632) of action is sustained without leave to amend as to Wells Fargo because HOLA preempts this state law as to the FSB that initially distributed this loan. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 4th cause of action (Fraud) and 5th cause of action (Negligent Misrepresentation) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead further facts with sufficient specificity as to these defendants. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 6th cause of action (Violation of the Covenant of Good faith) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show the Defendants had a duty to Plaintiff in the underlying loan documents. Plaintiff has sufficient plead facts to toll the Statute of Limitations.=(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ELSY M TADEO et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. Successor By Merger Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 18, 2011, line 6. Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. Successor By Merger Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint. Defendant¿s Motion to Strike the punitive damages claims is granted. No Opposition was filed. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JUANA PASCUBELLO VS. URBAN STRATEGIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Juana Pascubello'S Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Case # Cgc 11 509678 With Cgc-11-509681
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 18, 2011, line 7. Plaintiff Juana Pascubello'S Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Case # CGC 11 509678 With CGC-11-509681. Plaintiff's motion for consolidation is denied. The causes of action are completely different and the prejudice to defendants far outweigh any judicial economy to be gained by consolidation. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: FARAH SMOKE SHOP VS. CALIFORNIA STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition Of Writ Of Mandate To Review Administrative Decision By State Board Of Equalization
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 18, 2011, line 4. PETITIONER FARAH SMOKE SHOP Petition Of Writ Of Mandate To Review Administrative Decision By State Board Of Equalization. Petition for Writ of Mandate is denied. Petitioner failed to act expetitiously to obtain the administrative record as evidenced by counsel just filing a request on petitioner's behalf this week. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: RIVER PROPERTY TRUST VS. DAWN WIGGINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 18, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT DAWN WIGGINS Notice And Motion For Change Of Venue. Defendant's motion for change of venue is denied. Defendant's claim that she is not a resident of San Francisco directly conflicts with other court documents filed under penalty of perjury, where defendant claims she is a resident of San Francisco. =(302/LMG)
